title: Hieracium moscoviticum
taxonomy: Hieracium moscoviticum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is a type of hawkweed, which is a common name for plants in the Hieracium genus. The plant is native to Eastern Europe and has been introduced to other parts of the continent. Hieracium moscoviticum is classified as a perennial herb, typically growing to a height of 10-30 cm. The plant's classification is further detailed at the species level as Hieracium moscoviticum (Murray) Zahn.

intro: Hieracium moscoviticum, also known as Russian hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion-like Asteraceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and promoting immune system function. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium moscoviticum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium moscoviticum is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or tea to support immune system function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
